question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The population gap between California and Wyoming might exceed that between Virginia and Pennsylvania.
options.B: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.C: California and Wyoming have similar populations.
options.D: Virginia and Pennsylvania have very different population levels.

explanation: Virginia (12.8M) and Illinois (19.3M) have a 6.5M population difference, while California (5.8M) and New York (6.5M) have only a 0.7M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Virginia and Illinois into the same color bin (12-20M range), making their large difference appear similar, while California and New York appear more different due to different color bins.
